articles.headline: Iran rejects US peace proposal as regional conflict escalates
articles.summary: Hopes for a diplomatic end to the West Asia conflict dwindled as Tehran rejected a US peace proposal and maintained a list of strict demands. Iran asserted sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and demanded an end to the US naval blockade. Meanwhile, reports indicate US President Donald Trump is considering restarting military operations, while the Pentagon puts war costs at $29 billion.

articles.headline: States implement austerity measures following PM Modi's energy conservation appeal
articles.summary: Several Indian states including Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, and Madhya Pradesh have announced austerity measures to cut non-essential expenditure. Steps include a 50% reduction in official convoys, curbs on foreign travel, and shifting to virtual meetings. The moves follow Prime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to conserve fuel and foreign exchange amid rising global energy prices.

articles.headline: AIADMK factions split over support for ruling TVK alliance
articles.summary: The AIADMK faced a fresh crisis as two internal factions submitted rival claims to the assembly speaker. One group, led by CV Shanmugam, supports the ruling TVK alliance, while a faction led by OS Manian declared Edappadi K. Palaniswami as the legitimate legislature party chief. The split comes just before CM Joseph Vijay faces a trust vote on the floor of the House.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay appoints personal astrologer to government post
articles.summary: Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has appointed his long-time personal astrologer, Rickey Radhan Pandit Vetrivel, as an officer on special duty (OSD) in the CM’s office. The move drew criticism from allies Congress and VCK, who questioned the secular credentials of the appointment. Vetrivel is a popular astrologer who reportedly predicted Vijay's political rise.

articles.headline: Canada struggles to revoke 26/11 plotter Tahawwur Rana's citizenship
articles.summary: The Canadian government is facing legal difficulties in revoking the citizenship of Tahawwur Hussain Rana, a key figure in the 26/11 Mumbai attacks. Changes to legislation in 2016 removed terrorism as a ground for revocation. Authorities are now attempting to prove Rana obtained citizenship through deception regarding his residency in Ottawa, a legal process that could take a decade.

articles.headline: Calcutta High Court bench recuses from RG Kar murder case
articles.summary: A division bench of the Calcutta High Court recused itself from hearing a petition seeking a fresh investigation into the 2024 rape and murder of a trainee doctor at RG Kar Medical College. Justice Rajasekhar Mantha cited a heavy workload and the need for dedicated time for the high-profile case. The matter has been redirected to the Chief Justice's bench for further action.

articles.headline: High Court reserves order in Dhar Bhojshala religious nature dispute
articles.summary: The Indore bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court reserved its order on the religious nature of the Bhojshala Temple-Kamal Maula Mosque complex. The Muslim side challenged the ASI survey report as "biased" and lacking carbon dating. Hindu litigants argued for exclusive rights, citing evidence of a temple. The court concluded arguments from all parties following the submission of an ASI report.

articles.headline: St Stephen’s College appoints Susan Elias as its first woman principal
articles.summary: St Stephen’s College has appointed Professor Susan Elias as its first-ever woman principal in its 145-year history. A computer scientist, Elias plans to introduce AI certificate courses and focus on entrepreneurship. She succeeds Professor John Varghese and aims to transition the institution toward creating "job creators" and thought leaders in emerging technologies.

articles.headline: Wall collapse at Sangli temple kills six devotees and injures 12
articles.summary: At least six people were killed and 12 injured when a wall and tin sheds collapsed near a temple in Motewadi village, Sangli district, Maharashtra. The incident occurred amid gusty winds while approximately 350 devotees were present. Local authorities and police are investigating whether poor construction quality contributed to the collapse during the rescue operations.

articles.headline: Indian and Singaporean firms charged over US bridge collapse
articles.summary: Synergy Marine Pte Ltd (Singapore) and Synergy Maritime Pte Ltd (India) have been indicted on criminal charges in the US over the 2024 Dali ship crash that destroyed a bridge in Maryland. The companies and an Indian national, Radhakrishnan Nair, are accused of conspiracy to defraud and making false statements to federal investigators. The accident resulted in six deaths.

articles.headline: Sergio Ramos agrees to $470 million deal to buy Sevilla FC
articles.summary: Former Spain defender Sergio Ramos is reportedly set to purchase 80% of the shares of his boyhood club, Sevilla FC, for approximately 400 million euros ($470 million). Partnering with Five Eleven Capital, the deal would make him the major shareholder. The transaction requires final approval from LaLiga and the Spanish National Sports Council before completion.

articles.headline: PCOS renamed to PMOS to reflect broader metabolic health impact
articles.summary: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is being renamed to Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS) following a global medical effort. Researchers state the new name better reflects the condition's wide-ranging hormonal and metabolic impacts, rather than just ovarian cysts. PMOS affects 170 million women worldwide and is a leading cause of infertility and cardiovascular risks.

articles.headline: Explosives found near PM Modi's convoy route in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Karnataka police discovered gelatine sticks, batteries, and wires near a route used by Prime Minister Narendra Modi during his recent visit to Bengaluru. While not a functional IED, the circuit-like assembly found in a zip-lock cover has triggered a multi-agency investigation. The NIA is expected to join the probe to determine if the materials were planted to cause a security scare.

articles.headline: Karnataka seeks Supreme Court extension for Bengaluru civic elections
articles.summary: The Karnataka government and State Election Commission have approached the Supreme Court seeking more time to conduct Bengaluru civic polls. They cited manpower shortages caused by Census 2026 work and electoral roll revisions. The state is requesting a postponement until September, despite previous court directions to hold elections by the end of June for the newly created Greater Bengaluru Authority.

articles.headline: Union Minister Kumaraswamy approves ₹1,232 crore for Karnataka EV charging
articles.summary: Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy announced a ₹1,232.6 crore outlay for electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure in Karnataka. This includes the installation of 1,243 EV chargers. Nationwide, the Ministry of Heavy Industries has approved proposals worth ₹50,386 crore under the PM E-DRIVE scheme, covering 4,874 chargers across various states to make the automotive sector more competitive.

articles.headline: Two children die in fire at makeshift shed in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Two siblings, aged three and five, died in a fire at a makeshift shed in Kothanur, Bengaluru. The children were alone when the fire broke out, suffering from asphyxiation and severe burn injuries. Their parents were daily wage workers from Raichur. Police are investigating the cause of the fire, which produced dense smoke that prevented neighbors from rescuing the children in time.

articles.headline: Senior Maoist leader Narahari surrenders to Telangana police
articles.summary: Pasanuri Narahari, a Central Committee member of the proscribed CPI (Maoist), surrendered to Telangana police alongside his wife. Narahari was one of the last few active underground cadres from the state. His surrender marks a significant blow to the organization's regional structure. Since March 2024, a total of 818 Maoists have surrendered to authorities in the region.

articles.headline: Bandi Sanjay's son seeks pre-arrest bail in POCSO case
articles.summary: Bhageerath, son of Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, moved the Telangana High Court for anticipatory bail in a POCSO case. He is accused of sexually harassing a minor girl but alleges the family is blackmailing him for ₹5 crore. Police have launched a manhunt for Bhageerath, who is currently absconding. His father claims the case is a political attempt to frame his family.

articles.headline: Four students killed by lightning strike at Kerala viewpoint
articles.summary: Four men were killed and three others injured after lightning struck a popular tourist viewpoint on Panthallur hill in Malappuram, Kerala. The victims were part of a group of seven students trekking in the area. The three survivors are reported to be in stable condition. Local authorities identified the deceased as Rahees, Fahadh, Vishnu Varma, and Siyad.

articles.headline: Supreme Court examine delays in UP's criminal justice system
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has turned its focus on the massive pendency in Uttar Pradesh’s criminal justice system after quashing a 35-year-old case. The bench noted that a trial lasting three decades for minor offences violates Article 21. The court has sought state-wide data on pending bail applications, judicial vacancies, and the number of undertrials held for excessive periods.

articles.headline: Wife’s refusal to relocate for career is not cruelty, rules SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a woman’s refusal to sacrifice her professional career to relocate with her husband does not amount to cruelty or desertion. Overturning a High Court order, the bench called the expectation "feudalistic" and "archaic." The court emphasized that marriage does not extinguish a woman's intellectual identity or her right to professional aspirations.

articles.headline: Defence Secretary outlines roadmap for Indo-Pacific power projection
articles.summary: Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh stated India is on course to project power across the Indo-Pacific by 2047. The 'Vision 2047' roadmap involves transitioning the military into a technologically advanced, integrated force. Key goals include the Mission Sudarshan Chakra missile shield, a dedicated drone force, and space and cyber commands to protect India's global economic footprint.

articles.headline: US imposes visa sanctions on Indian pharmacy owner for fentanyl
articles.summary: The US has imposed visa restrictions on the owner and 13 associates of India-based KS International Traders. The firm is accused of trafficking illicit fentanyl and selling counterfeit prescriptions to Americans. The State Department designated the pharmacy's activities as a threat involving weapons of mass destruction, following an executive order targeting global synthetic drug trades.

articles.headline: Iraq and Pakistan sign energy deals with Iran despite conflict
articles.summary: Iraq and Pakistan have signed agreements with Iran to ship oil and liquefied natural gas through the Strait of Hormuz. The deals demonstrate Iran's continued influence over regional energy flows. Iraq secured safe passage for crude carriers, while Pakistan seeks to act as a mediator in the US-Iran conflict while addressing its own internal energy security and surging fuel costs.

articles.headline: Google to deeply embed Gemini AI into Android OS
articles.summary: Google plans a major overhaul of the Android operating system by integrating Gemini AI into third-party apps. The new "Gemini Intelligence" layer aims to transform phones into active agents capable of automating tasks like booking tickets or translating spoken messages. The update will roll out in waves, starting with flagship devices like the Pixel 10 and Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra.

articles.headline: Nahid Rana leads Bangladesh to historic Test win over Pakistan
articles.summary: Pacer Nahid Rana claimed 5-40 as Bangladesh defeated Pakistan by 104 runs in the first Test at Dhaka. This marks Bangladesh's first-ever Test victory against Pakistan on home soil. Chasing 268, Pakistan collapsed to 163 all out in the final session. Bangladesh captain Najmul Hossain Shanto equalled the national record for most Test wins by a captain in just 17 matches.

articles.headline: Jannik Sinner equals Djokovic's record with 31st Masters win
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner defeated Andrea Pellegrino 6-2, 6-3 to reach the Italian Open quarterfinals. The win is his 31st consecutive victory in Masters Series events, equaling the record set by Novak Djokovic in 2011. Sinner remains undefeated in Masters tournaments since October, continuing a dominant season that has seen him move closer to the top world ranking.

articles.headline: High Court notice over FIFA World Cup 2026 free-to-air broadcast
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court issued notice to the Centre and Prasar Bharati regarding a petition seeking free-to-air broadcast of the FIFA World Cup 2026. Currently, no broadcaster has secured Indian media rights for the event. The petitioner argued that India is one of FIFA's largest markets and millions of citizens would be deprived of viewing the tournament without public broadcast access.

articles.headline: Top 16 IT Firms to Return Rs 1.3 Lakh Crore to Shareholders
articles.summary: India's leading 16 listed information technology companies have announced a record Rs 1.3 trillion payout through dividends and share buybacks for FY26. This is a 36.3% increase compared to the previous year's Rs 95,400 crore. Payouts rose despite a slowing net profit growth of 3.5%, the slowest in eight years, as companies prioritized shareholder returns over cash reserves.

articles.headline: Private Equity Firms Sell Go Shares Worth Rs 5,326 Crore
articles.summary: Peak XV Partners, Sequoia Capital, and Ribbit Capital sold a 4.71% stake in Billionbrains Garage Ventures, the parent company of stockbroking platform Go, for Rs 5,326 crore via block deals. The shares were sold at Rs 180.4 each. Following the deal, Go's stock closed at Rs 183 on the BSE, down 5.5% from the previous close.

articles.headline: Hindustan Zinc Signs Pact for Rajasthan Zinc Wire Unit
articles.summary: Vedanta group company Hindustan Zinc Limited has signed an agreement with Group Nirmala to establish a zinc wire manufacturing unit in Rajasthan. The project aims to enhance value-added product capacity within the state. Financial details of the deal were not disclosed. The company's stock closed 2.0% higher at Rs 641.6 following the announcement.

articles.headline: Manipal Health Acquires Mumbai Hospital Property for Rs 495 Crore
articles.summary: Temasek-backed Manipal Health Enterprises has purchased a hospital and land in Andheri, Mumbai, from Kuberandani Hospitals for approximately Rs 495 crore. The deal includes 752.77 square meters of land and a building with a carpet area of 20,663.80 square meters. Manipal had previously paid Rs 413 crore in 2024 for parts of the same assets, taking the total deal value to over Rs 900 crore.

articles.headline: Fractal Explores Enterprise AI Partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic
articles.summary: AI solutions firm Fractal is seeking deeper partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic to integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) into enterprise workflows. Co-founder Srikanth Velamakanni noted that while the initial hype has cooled, the demand for high-quality data and security in AI adoption remains high. Fractal aims to help clients automate complex workflows using these advanced models.

articles.headline: GJC Supports PM's Appeal to Limit Gold Imports
articles.summary: The All India Gem and Jewellery Domestic Council (GJC) has urged the government to strengthen the Gold Monetization Scheme (GMS) following PM Modi's appeal to reduce foreign exchange spending. Chairman Rajesh Rokde suggested that using existing household gold could stabilize the rupee. He recommended reconsidering 'destination weddings' abroad to save foreign currency reserves.

articles.headline: Bajaj Group Celebrates 100 Years of Business Operations
articles.summary: The Bajaj Group is marking its centenary year, evolving from a scooter manufacturer into one of India’s most successful retail lenders. Since 2000, the group’s combined revenue has grown from Rs 4,782 crore to nearly Rs 2 lakh crore in FY25. Bajaj Finserv and Bajaj Finance now contribute 68.3% of the group's total revenue, overshadowing its traditional auto business.

articles.headline: Nilesh Shah Advises Portfolio Diversification Over Aggressive Buying
articles.summary: In an interview, Kotak Mahindra AMC MD Nilesh Shah warned that current market conditions are not suitable for aggressive equity buying. He suggested investors diversify into Special Investment Funds, REITs, and InvITs. Citing the West Asia crisis, he noted that rising oil prices act like 'Rahu Kaal' for the economy, impacting inflation, CAD, and interest rates.

articles.headline: Market Crash Wipes Out Rs 11.3 Lakh Crore in One Session
articles.summary: Indian equity markets saw a major sell-off as the Sensex crashed 1,456 points (1.92%) and Nifty fell 1.83%. Investor wealth worth Rs 11.3 lakh crore was wiped out in a single day, taking the two-day loss to Rs 17.44 lakh crore. Panic was driven by rising oil prices and a major correction in IT stocks following OpenAI’s entry into the consulting and engineering services space.

articles.headline: States to Get Financial Incentives for Renewable Energy PPAs
articles.summary: Renewable Energy Minister Pralhad Joshi announced that states will receive fiscal incentives for signing Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) for green energy. This new policy, approved by the Finance and Power ministries, will link renewable energy adoption to interest-free loans. The government is also addressing quality issues in imported solar modules with a new 10-year warranty framework.

articles.headline: Coal India to Explore Lithium Blocks in Chile
articles.summary: Coal India Limited (CIL) is planning to diversify by establishing subsidiaries in Chile and Singapore to explore lithium, copper, and cobalt. Chairman B. Sairam confirmed that the company has identified a lithium block in Chile and is awaiting final approval from the Chilean government. CIL aims to integrate into the global critical minerals value chain within the next 2-3 years.

articles.headline: General Insurance Council to Empanel 5,000 Hospitals by 2027
articles.summary: The General Insurance Council (GIC) aims to increase the number of hospitals in its common empanelment list to 5,000 by the end of FY27. Currently, about 2,000 hospitals are linked through the common digital platform. This initiative allows hospitals to apply once to be on the panels of all registered general and standalone health insurance companies in India.

articles.headline: India Targets Rs 8.8 Lakh Crore Defense Production by 2047
articles.summary: Defense Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh stated that India aims to increase its annual domestic defense production six-fold to Rs 8.8 lakh crore by 2047. Defense exports are projected to grow nine-fold to Rs 2.8 lakh crore. Under 'Vision 2047', the country targets becoming a fully integrated, self-reliant, and Indo-Pacific focused military power by 2040.

articles.headline: Assam BJP to Introduce New Land Policy for Industry
articles.summary: BJP State President Samik Bhattacharya announced that the Assam government will introduce a new land policy to facilitate industrial growth. The policy aims to remove obstacles like the Land Ceiling Act and allow companies to acquire larger tracts of land, with provisions to return 10% of developed land to original owners. The focus is on modernizing agriculture and enabling SEZs.

articles.headline: PM Modi to Focus on Fujairah Bypass During UAE Visit
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi's upcoming visit to Abu Dhabi will focus on strategic energy cooperation. Discussions include a plan to bypass the Strait of Hormuz by utilizing the Fujairah port on the Gulf of Oman for oil and LPG shipments. This would require UAE to expand terminal capacity and build a new pipeline from Abu Dhabi's Habshan terminal to Fujairah to ensure India's energy security.

articles.headline: IMD Launches AI-Based Monsoon Forecast Models
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has introduced its first AI-based monsoon forecast model to provide block-level accuracy. Earth Sciences Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh stated the model could provide specific forecasts four weeks in advance for 3,196 blocks. Additionally, a high-resolution pilot project for Uttar Pradesh was launched to track small-scale rainfall variations.

articles.headline: Heatwave Warning Issued for Western and Central India
articles.summary: The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert for the next 4-5 days across Western Rajasthan, Eastern Rajasthan, Central India, Gujarat, and Maharashtra. Meanwhile, conditions are becoming favorable for the onset of the Southwest Monsoon in parts of the South Bay of Bengal and the Andaman Sea by the end of this week.

articles.headline: Crude spike sparks rupee, market rout; investors lose $115 billion
articles.summary: Indian markets suffered a massive blow as rising crude oil prices and West Asia tensions triggered heavy selling. The Sensex shed 1,456 points while the Nifty fell 1.83%. Approximately $115 billion in market wealth was wiped out in a single day as the rupee slumped to a record low of 95.73 per dollar, prompting concerns over macroeconomic stability.

articles.headline: NEET test cancelled, new dates to be notified soon
articles.summary: The government has cancelled the NEET (UG) 2026 medical entrance exam following allegations of paper leaks and an FIR filed by the CBI. The National Testing Agency (NTA) stated the move ensures transparency and the sanctity of the exam. New dates will be announced within 7-10 days, affecting over 22 lakh candidates across the country.

articles.headline: No ATF relief: Air India looks to suspend long-haul flights
articles.summary: Air India is considering suspending several long-haul international flights until August due to high aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices and lack of relief from oil companies. Routes to Chicago, Newark, and various Australian cities may face frequency reductions as fuel costs now account for a substantial portion of the airline's total operating expenses.

articles.headline: Canara Bank CEO expects healthy rise in savings and retail deposits
articles.summary: Canara Bank CEO Hardeep Singh Ahluwalia expects a healthy rise in savings and retail term deposits in the coming quarters. The bank is sharpening its focus on low-cost liabilities and high-yield retail lending to sustain margins. He noted a shift in consumer behavior, with individuals moving funds from volatile equity markets back into the banking channel.

articles.headline: Tata Chemicals CEO Mukundan takes over as CII president
articles.summary: Tata Chemicals MD & CEO R Mukundan has assumed office as the President of the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) for the 2026-27 period. He takes over from Suchitra K Ella. Mukundan will lead the industry body during a period focused on navigating global economic shifts and domestic growth strategies.

articles.headline: Legal notice disputes 1989 transfer of Tata Sons shares to Naval Tata
articles.summary: A legal notice has been sent to senior trustees of Tata Trusts challenging the 1989 transfer of 833 Tata Sons shares to the late Naval Tata. The complainant alleges the transfer was unauthorized and lacked a valid board resolution. The notice claims these shares are now worth several thousand crores of rupees and seeks to void the transaction.

articles.headline: India's forex reserves shrink 5% since March on oil import costs
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves have fallen to $690.7 billion, a 5.19% decrease since March 2026. The decline is attributed to the high cost of oil imports and the RBI's intervention to support the rupee. India is among the highest-reporting EM economies for reserves but has faced one of the worst currency depreciations in the recent period.

articles.headline: Tractor sector saw 27% jump in sales in April
articles.summary: Domestic tractor sales grew 27% year-on-year in April 2026, reaching 1,05,021 units. The growth was supported by favorable farm sentiment, improved rural liquidity, and lower GST rates on certain components. Analysts expect a moderate monsoon and high base effects to result in a more subdued growth trajectory for the remainder of the fiscal year.

articles.headline: Maruti to back 6 start-ups for AI-based technology solutions
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki India has selected six start-ups from its Accelerator Program to co-create AI-based and technology-led solutions. The start-ups will focus on improving productivity, warehouse management, and safety in vehicle production. Maruti will support these ventures through its innovation program to enhance customer experience and operational efficiency.

articles.headline: RIL's Mappedu Logistics Park renamed Chennai Global Logistics Park
articles.summary: Reliance Industries' multi-modal logistics park project at Mappedu near Chennai has been renamed Chennai Global Logistics Park. The project is being developed in collaboration with DP World. Sources indicate that various global logistics firms have already acquired significant space in the park, which aims to enhance connectivity and warehousing infrastructure in the region.

articles.headline: Tata Power Q4 revenue dips 12.8% on Mundra unit closure
articles.summary: Tata Power reported a 12.8% fall in Q4 FY26 revenue to ₹14,900 crore, primarily due to the temporary closure of its Mundra power plant. Despite the revenue dip, net profit for the quarter rose by 4.5% to ₹996 crore. The company saw strong growth in its solar EPC business and utility-scale projects, helping offset the decline in thermal generation.

articles.headline: Adani Ports enters Europe offshore services market with Astro acquisition
articles.summary: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) has entered the European offshore services market through its subsidiary Astro Offshore. The move follows the acquisition of a 100% stake in Astro, which provides specialized vessels for underwater construction and subsea projects. The partnership aims to build a global platform for offshore marine services.

articles.headline: Modi's UAE visit to focus on energy security and trade: MEA
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's upcoming visit to the United Arab Emirates will focus on strengthening energy security and exploring new investment opportunities. Discussions will include securing crude oil and LNG supplies amid West Asia tensions. The visit aims to build on the India-UAE bilateral trade, which surpassed $100 billion in the 2025-26 fiscal year.

articles.headline: Draft of new employment policy to be released soon for feedback
articles.summary: The Union government is finalizing a draft for a new national employment policy, which will be released shortly for stakeholder feedback. The policy aims to address job creation, employer-linked financial incentives, and labor reforms. It will focus on bridging the gap between jobseekers and industries through structured government-led initiatives.

articles.headline: Govt weighs opening ballistic missile production to private sector
articles.summary: Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh indicated that the government is considering opening up ballistic missile manufacturing to the private sector. This move follows the decision to allow private participation in the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program. The initiative aims to boost domestic defense production to a target of ₹8.8 lakh crore.

articles.headline: Adani group stocks end lower in weak market sentiment
articles.summary: Shares of Adani group companies ended lower on Tuesday, with Adani Green and Adani Power declining nearly 6% and 5.8% respectively. The slump tracked a broader market rout triggered by rising crude prices and foreign fund outflows. The combined market valuation of the group's listed entities stood at approximately ₹16.70 lakh crore at the close of trade.

articles.headline: SAT upholds SEBI order on disgorgement of unlawful gains
articles.summary: The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) has upheld a SEBI order requiring the disgorgement of ₹21 lakh in unlawful gains from a fraudulent trading case. The tribunal rejected the appellants' plea to offset the amount with taxes already paid on those gains. The ruling clarifies that tax payments on disputed income do not exempt individuals from regulatory disgorgement orders.

articles.headline: JSW Energy slumps 7% as brokerages turn cautious on growth
articles.summary: Shares of JSW Energy fell 8% after reporting a 29% decline in consolidated net profit for Q4. Analysts from several brokerages turned cautious, citing high valuations and slower-than-expected capacity expansion. Despite the current slump, the company maintains its long-term target of reaching 20 GW capacity and 40 GWh of storage by 2030.

articles.headline: Analysts see headroom in Indian Hotels post strong Q4 results
articles.summary: Despite a volatile market, analysts maintain a positive outlook for Indian Hotels Company Ltd following its strong Q4 performance. The company reported a 32% growth in consolidated net profit, driven by strong demand in the domestic leisure and business segments. Experts believe the company has significant headroom for growth due to its expansion plans and portfolio diversification.

articles.headline: MobiKwik posts profit for second straight quarter in Q4
articles.summary: Fintech firm MobiKwik has reported a profit of ₹4.4 crore for the fourth quarter, its second consecutive profitable period. The company saw a 58% growth in its Gross Merchandise Value (GMV). Revenue from operations grew 8% year-on-year. MobiKwik attributed the turnaround to growth in its payments business and financial services through its mobile app.

articles.headline: CPPIB India to acquire stake in Ctrl S Datacenters
articles.summary: The Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPPIB) has received approval from the CCI to acquire a stake in Hyderabad-based Ctrl S Datacenters. CPPIB will invest via its subsidiary, India Private Holdings Inc. This investment highlights the growing interest of global institutional investors in India's expanding digital infrastructure and data center market.

articles.headline: Syrma SGS expects 30% revenue growth in FY27
articles.summary: Electronics manufacturing services firm Syrma SGS expects its revenue to grow by 30% in the 2026-27 fiscal year. The company aims for exports to cross ₹1,500 crore, supported by its new manufacturing facility in Chennai. Managing Director Jasbir Singh Gujral noted that while the West Asia crisis poses logistics challenges, the company is seeing strong demand for AI-led products.

articles.headline: Rupee slumps to a record low of 95.73 per dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit an all-time low of 95.7375 against the US dollar on Tuesday, falling 31 paise from its previous close. The decline was fueled by a spike in global crude oil prices, equity market rout, and foreign fund outflows. The RBI is believed to have intervened in the forex market to prevent a further sharp depreciation of the currency.

articles.headline: India sees steady mining growth; J&K opens limestone blocks sale
articles.summary: India's mining sector continues to show steady growth despite global headwinds. The government has launched the second tranche of e-auctions for 12 limestone blocks in Jammu and Kashmir to attract private investment. Union Minister Pralhad Joshi stated that the move aims to ensure a stable supply of minerals for the domestic industry and boost regional economic development.

articles.headline: PM Internship Scheme expanded to include final-year diploma students
articles.summary: The Union government has expanded the PM Internship Scheme to include final-year diploma students, aiming to enhance their employability. Previously restricted to graduates, the scheme now allows more students to gain industry experience at top companies. The government aims to provide 1 crore internships over five years to bridge the skill gap in the workforce.

articles.headline: Net holdings in gold ETFs down 80% since January 1
articles.summary: Net holdings in gold exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have dropped by nearly 80% since the start of the year as investors booked profits amid surging gold prices. Global net outflows from gold ETFs totaled $1.72 billion in early May. In India, however, inflows into gold ETFs remained positive, bucking the global trend as local investors sought a hedge against market volatility.

articles.headline: Models suggest El Nino could be of at least moderate strength
articles.summary: Climate models indicate that the current El Nino event could be of at least moderate strength, potentially impacting the upcoming South-West monsoon. International weather agencies have noted rising sea surface temperatures in the central Pacific. The IMD is closely monitoring the situation as a strong El Nino often leads to deficient rainfall in the Indian subcontinent.

articles.headline: IMD launches pilot weather forecast within 1 km radius in UP
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has launched a pilot project in Uttar Pradesh to provide localized weather forecasts within a 1 km radius. The advanced system aims to provide more accurate data to farmers and disaster management agencies. The IMD plans a national rollout of this high-resolution forecasting service within the next three years.

articles.headline: SC: Right to Freedom of Religion cannot be breached for reform
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that the right to freedom of religion cannot be breached in the name of social reform unless it violates public order, morality, or health. The 5-judge Constitution Bench emphasized that the state must maintain a neutral stance and not interfere in essential religious practices. The ruling came during a hearing on discriminatory practices in religious places.

articles.headline: Govt unveils Unified Bharat e-Charge to streamline EV charging
articles.summary: The Union government has launched the Unified Bharat e-Charge (UBC) platform, a UPI-like system to streamline electric vehicle charging across India. The platform allows EV users to find, book, and pay for charging sessions at any station through a single app. This initiative aims to reduce range anxiety and accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles in the country.

articles.headline: GST Council Reviews Tax Status for Subscription-Based Ride-Hailing Platforms
articles.summary: The GST Council is investigating if ride-hailing apps using a subscription model should pay GST. Unlike commission-based firms, subscription-based apps claim they only connect drivers and riders. A panel is reviewing Section 9(5) of the CGST Act to ensure tax parity between different business models and prevent potential tax base erosion in the digital economy.

articles.headline: Report Shows Informal Sector Businesses Lag in Internet Usage Rates
articles.summary: New government data reveals that 17 Indian states report internet use by less than half of their informal businesses. While Sikkim shows high adoption at 93.4%, states like Uttar Pradesh and Odisha lag significantly at 18.7% and 26.4% respectively. The data highlights a digital divide in the push toward a digital economy across manufacturing and trade sectors.

articles.headline: West Asia War Strains India’s Macros Forcing Shift Toward Demand Curtailment
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict has pushed India's macro risks higher, with the rupee hitting record lows and the current account deficit expected to reach a 14-year high of 2.2% in FY27. PM Narendra Modi has called for national austerity measures, including reducing fuel consumption and gold imports, to manage fiscal strain as the RBI spends billions to defend the currency.

articles.headline: Indian Stocks Slump as West Asia Tensions Trigger Massive Wealth Wipeout
articles.summary: The Sensex tumbled 1.9% and Nifty 50 fell 1.8% on Tuesday, wiping out ₹10.95 trillion in investor wealth. Tensions in West Asia, surging crude oil prices, and persistent foreign fund outflows rattled markets. The India VIX 'fear gauge' jumped 5% to 19.46, reflecting heightened volatility expectations as investors reacted to calls for economic restraint.

articles.headline: IT Firms Tweak Billing Models as Artificial Intelligence Gains Ground
articles.summary: Indian IT services firms are moving away from traditional hourly billing to token-based and subscription models as AI automates software tasks. Companies like Cognizant, Tech Mahindra, and LTM are introducing AI-infused rate cards and prepaid credits. The shift aims to provide clients with more predictable outcome-based pricing as AI agents take over human-led processes.

articles.headline: Food Regulator Issues Notice to The Whole Truth Over Labeling Claims
articles.summary: FSSAI has issued a show-cause notice to Fitshit Health Solutions-owned The Whole Truth over 'no added sugar' claims on chocolate products sweetened with date powder. Regulatory rules prohibit such claims if ingredients containing sugars are used as substitutes. The company has since committed to updating its labels to 'sweetened with dates' following the complaint.

articles.headline: Investors Bet on Commercial Lawsuits as Niche Alternative Asset Class
articles.summary: Third-party litigation funding is emerging in India, with firms like Five Rivers, LegalPay, and ELF Partners financing lawsuits for a share of settlements. Five Rivers is raising a $25-50 million fund targeting returns over 30%. The practice, established in the US, helps claimants access high-quality legal teams by covering litigation costs and upfront capital.

articles.headline: Elon Musk’s Grok AI Lags Behind Competitors Despite SpaceX Support
articles.summary: Data shows Elon Musk’s AI model, Grok, is losing ground to rivals ChatGPT and Claude, with downloads falling to 8.3 million in April. While SpaceX has agreed to rent computing power to Anthropic, Grok’s adoption in enterprise sectors remains low. Users cite a preference for more 'truth-seeking' or capable models as the AI race intensifies globally.

articles.headline: West Bengal Expected to Join Digital Agriculture Mission for Farm Reforms
articles.summary: West Bengal is set to integrate with the Centre’s Digital Agriculture Mission to create digital identities for millions of farmers. The move will streamline subsidy delivery and improve access to insurance and credit through the AgriStack platform. Bengal is currently the only state yet to join the mission, which aims to enhance data-driven policymaking.

articles.headline: IMD to Launch AI-Powered Sub-District Level Monsoon Onset Forecasts
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department is rolling out an AI-based system to predict monsoon arrival at the block level for over 3,000 sub-districts. Developed with IITM Pune, the tool provides probabilistic forecasts 10 days in advance. This upgrade aims to help farmers in rain-fed regions time their sowing more accurately amid a predicted below-normal monsoon season.

articles.headline: Centre Plans Decentralized Onion Procurement and Storage to Control Prices
articles.summary: The Indian government is developing a plan to help states locally procure and store onions using scientific techniques. The strategy aims to reduce wastage, lower transport costs from major producers like Maharashtra, and create buffer stocks closer to demand centers. This decentralized approach is designed to stabilize retail prices and improve farmer realizations.

articles.headline: Indian Banks Resume Bullion Imports After 3% Customs Levy Agreement
articles.summary: Domestic lenders have resumed gold and silver imports after a month-long halt. Banks agreed to pay a 3% customs levy that had previously stalled shipments. The resumption follows clarifications on trade terms, ensuring steady supply for the domestic jewelry sector despite broader government calls for reduced gold consumption to protect forex reserves.

articles.headline: Actis LLP Considers Exit From Listed Indian Roads Platform Nxt-Infra Trust
articles.summary: Infrastructure investor Actis is exploring a partial or full exit from its listed Nxt-Infra Trust. The trust owns 14 road assets across five states with an enterprise value of approximately ₹6,030 crore. Ambit has been hired to find buyers among institutional investors. Actis owns 86% of the InvIT, which has seen buoyant interest due to stable yields.

articles.headline: Maruti Suzuki Selects Six Startups to Enhance Manufacturing Safety and Efficiency
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki has chosen six startups from its 10th accelerator cohort to co-create tech-based business solutions. The innovations focus on improving plant safety, material traceability, and customer experience. The initiative is part of the automaker's flagship programme to integrate new-age technology into its manufacturing and product development processes.

articles.headline: TikTok Challenges EU ‘Gatekeeper’ Designation at Top European Court
articles.summary: ByteDance-owned TikTok made a final legal attempt to scrap its 'gatekeeper' status under the EU Digital Markets Act. The designation forces tech giants to meet tougher competition standards. TikTok argues that its market impact is primarily in Asia and that it faces different competitive dynamics than rivals like Google and Meta, who also carry the tag.

articles.headline: ABB India Shares Drop as Ebitda Margins Erode Despite Record Orders
articles.summary: ABB India reported its highest-ever quarterly order inflow of ₹4,280 crore, but shares fell 10% as Ebitda margins dropped to 12.8%. Stiff competition and rising input costs due to the West Asia conflict impacted profitability. The company is expanding capacity in renewable energy and metro rail sectors, though full margin recovery is expected to take several quarters.

articles.headline: NCDEX Plans Equity Derivatives Launch and Mutual Fund Platform Entry
articles.summary: The National Commodity & Derivatives Exchange (NCDEX) aims to launch equity derivatives and a cash segment within 12 months. The exchange, traditionally focused on agri-commodities, received in-principle Sebi approval to diversify. Plans include a mutual fund platform as a precursor to broader equity market expansion to build distribution infrastructure.

articles.headline: IT Stocks Hit as OpenAI Enters Software Services Market
articles.summary: Shares of top Indian IT firms like TCS, Infosys, and Wipro fell significantly after OpenAI announced a new joint venture for AI deployment. The venture, involving 19 private equity firms, will hire engineers to embed AI directly into client systems. This move bypasses traditional tech outsourcers, raising concerns about their relevance in the rapidly evolving AI era.

articles.headline: Retail Investors Shift to Large-Cap Stocks Amid Market Volatility
articles.summary: Indian retail investors turned selective in the March quarter, favoring large-cap firms like HDFC Bank and Hindustan Copper. Data from 4,489 listed firms shows that while participation in small-caps weakened, HDFC Bank added over 5.7 lakh new retail shareholders. Analysts suggest a 'safety-first' approach as volatility erodes wealth in riskier micro-cap segments.

articles.headline: Tractor and Entry-Level Motorcycle Sales Signal Rural Economic Stress
articles.summary: Leading tractor makers Mahindra and Escorts Kubota expect a slowdown in sales this year, signaling rural distress. Factors include a predicted below-average monsoon and rising vehicle prices due to the West Asia conflict. Bajaj Auto also flagged a decline in entry-level motorcycle sales, a trend that may spill over into the fast-moving consumer goods sector.

articles.headline: Billionaire Uday Kotak Warns of Big Oil Price Shock for India
articles.summary: Uday Kotak cautioned that India must prepare for a significant energy price shock as the West Asia war impacts supply chains. He noted that retail fuel prices, which have remained steady for 18 months, are likely to rise soon. Kotak supported PM Modi's call for austerity, stating that the economic impact on the current account and inflation is imminent and large.

articles.headline: Indian Micro-Drama Startups Seek $50 Million in New Funding Wave
articles.summary: Startups like Flick TV, Rigi, and Dashverse Reels are seeking $25-50 million in venture capital to capitalize on the short-form episodic content boom. India’s micro-drama market is projected to reach $4.5 billion by 2030. Investors are drawn to AI-enabled production and microtransaction models that cater to 100 million monthly active users on mobile platforms.

articles.headline: Dr Reddy’s to Launch Generic Semaglutide Weight-Loss Drug in Canada
articles.summary: Dr Reddy’s Laboratories is preparing to launch a generic version of the blockbuster drug semaglutide in Canada following regulatory approval. The company is also rolling out oral semaglutide tablets in India. The drugmaker plans to enter over 80 countries with GLP-1 products as patents for original weight-loss and diabetes treatments begin to expire.

articles.headline: U.A.E. Secretly Conducted Military Strikes on Iranian Oil Refinery
articles.summary: Reports indicate the United Arab Emirates carried out military strikes on an Iranian refinery on Lavan Island in early April. The move marks a shift in the U.A.E.'s strategy to respond kinetically to Iranian hostility. While not publicly acknowledged, the strikes occurred after Iran targeted Gulf infrastructure with drones, hammering regional air traffic and tourism.

articles.headline: U.S. and Iran Locked in Diplomatic Stalemate Over Nuclear Program
articles.summary: The U.S. and Iran remain in a 'gray zone' that is neither peace nor war, with a one-month cease-fire on life support. President Trump stated he will not back off from pressing Iran to abandon its nuclear program, while Iran maintains its regime is intact. Both sides continue dueling blockades in the Strait of Hormuz, with little indication of a strategic compromise.

articles.headline: Government Approves ₹503 Crore for Public Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure
articles.summary: The heavy industries ministry cleared funds for 4,874 public EV charging stations under the PM E-Drive scheme. Part of a larger ₹2,000-crore plan, the rollout aims to accelerate EV adoption and strengthen energy security amid West Asia tensions. Karnataka is slated to receive 1,243 stations as the government targets 72,000 chargers nationwide.

articles.headline: Eli Lilly Pauses Indian Obesity Campaign After Regulatory Warning
articles.summary: Eli Lilly has halted its 'We Know Now' obesity awareness campaign in India following a warning from the Drugs Controller General. The regulator cited potential violations of rules against advertising prescription medicines to consumers. Lilly stated the pause was a matter of 'regulatory caution' while clarifying the ads did not name specific products.

articles.headline: Tata Power Quarterly Profit Slips Due to Gujarat Plant Shutdown
articles.summary: Tata Power reported a 4.5% drop in fourth-quarter net profit to ₹996 crore, impacted by the shutdown of its 4-gigawatt Mundra plant. The plant ceased operations after the government withdrew emergency compensation for expensive coal imports. Operations resumed in April following new directives to run at full capacity to meet rising summer energy demand.

articles.headline: Ford’s $3 Billion Michigan Battery Plant Uses Chinese Tech Licensing
articles.summary: Ford Motor Co. is nearing completion of its BlueOval Battery Park, utilizing technology from China's CATL through a licensing agreement. The deal allows Ford to own the plant and hire US workers while using Chinese battery chemistry. The project highlights the dilemma of using Chinese expertise to build local capacity without violating national security concerns.

articles.headline: DIY Investing Trends Drive Gen Z Toward Riskier Assets and Crypto
articles.summary: Generation Z investors now account for 38.4% of India’s equity investor base, favoring direct stocks, ETFs, and crypto over traditional savings. Data shows young salaried investors are increasing exposure to small-cap firms despite high risks. Experts warn that while engagement is high, social media-driven 'Fomo' and herd behavior often replace professional financial advice.

articles.headline: Asus Zenbook Duo Review: A Functional Dual-Screen Laptop Breakthrough
articles.summary: The new Asus Zenbook Duo (UX8407) features two 14-inch Oled displays and Intel’s Panther Lake chips, offering true all-day battery life for the first time in its class. Priced at ₹2,99,999, the device features a magnetic Bluetooth keyboard and enhanced internal graphics. While niche, it marks a significant evolution in making dual-screen productivity practical for mobile users.

articles.headline: Indian Stock Markets Plunge as Indices Drop Two Percent
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex dropped 1,456 points while the Nifty fell 436 points on Tuesday as market capitalisation fell by nearly ₹17 lakh crore over two days. The sell-off was triggered by escalating West Asia tensions and surging crude oil prices. The Indian rupee also hit a fresh record closing low of 95.62 against the US dollar amid concerns over the country's fiscal deficit.

articles.headline: Inflation Pressures Likely to Stall Discretionary Consumer Spending
articles.summary: A combination of high fuel costs and rising food prices is expected to hit consumer sentiment and discretionary spending. Companies in electronics and appliances fear a recovery stall as costs rise due to a weaker rupee and supply chain disruptions. Retail sales growth in India has slowed to 9-10% in the last six months, with price hikes of 15-20% expected for non-essential goods.

articles.headline: India Slams China for Shielding Terrorists Under Operation Sindoor
articles.summary: India has flagged the reputational cost of China supporting Pakistan-based terror infrastructure, following recent confrontations during Operation Sindoor. The External Affairs Ministry stated that reports corroborate previous information about Chinese engineers being present at Pakistani airbases and assisting fighter aircraft operations during the conflict.

articles.headline: Japanese Shipping Giant Mitsui OSK Lines Plans Ship Building in India
articles.summary: Mitsui OSK Lines (MOL), the world's second-largest ship owner, is exploring opportunities to build ships in India. The company is evaluating partnerships to build roll-on, roll-off terminals and strengthen inland logistics services. MOL currently has 13 vessels flying the Indian flag and aims to expand its role in the country's growing maritime capabilities.

articles.headline: Oil Marketing Companies Absorbing Losses is Long-Term Concern
articles.summary: Union Oil Minister Hardeep Singh Puri stated that state-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) cannot indefinitely absorb losses from selling fuel below market rates. While global prices remain volatile due to the West Asia crisis, the government is looking to reduce petrol and diesel consumption through green initiatives and public transport to shield consumers from sudden price hikes.

articles.headline: PMO Drafting Plan to Boost Foreign Direct Investment Flows
articles.summary: The Prime Minister’s Office is coordinating with various ministries to identify fresh opportunities to bolster foreign investment and exports. The plan includes easing FEMA rules to make bilateral investment treaties more investor-friendly. Niti Aayog is also identifying potential opportunities offered by Iran as India seeks to reduce its trade deficit and diversify trade partners.

articles.headline: Private Equity Investors See Up To 94x Returns in Groww
articles.summary: Block deal stake sales in Groww have delivered returns of up to 94x for early investors like Peak XV, Ribbit, and Y Combinator following the IPO lock-in expiry. Nearly 418.2 crore shares valued at approximately ₹8,000 crore became eligible for trading. Major institutional buyers included HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential, and the Norway sovereign wealth fund.

articles.headline: CBI Files FIR in NEET-UG Paper Leak Case
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has registered an FIR into the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case based on a complaint from the Union Education Ministry. The FIR alleges criminal conspiracy, cheating, and breach of trust. Special teams have been formed to investigate the leak, which reportedly originated from a coaching center network involving organized crime syndicates.

articles.headline: NTA Faces Scrutiny Over Repeated Marking Errors in Exams
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) is facing criticism for multiple errors in the NEET-UG exam, including instances where students were marked for the wrong bubble on OMR sheets. Reports suggest that as many as 19 errors occurred in the Chemistry section alone. The agency has been under fire for its perceived lack of efficiency and inability to conduct high-stakes exams securely.

articles.headline: Radhakrishnan Panel Suggestions for NTA Overhaul Not Yet Implemented
articles.summary: The K Radhakrishnan panel, formed to suggest reforms for the National Testing Agency, has seen most of its recommendations remain unimplemented. Key suggestions included a two-stage examination system, multistage authentication using biometrics, and a shift to computer-based testing. Out of 101 recommendations, only five have been fully implemented by the government so far.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Discusses Kerala Chief Minister Choice with Congress Leaders
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi held one-on-one meetings with Kerala Congress leaders to discuss the choice of a new Chief Minister for the state. The meetings aimed to gauge local sentiment and address recent lobbying efforts by various factions within the party. Leaders were asked to provide their individual views on the best candidate to lead the state government.

articles.headline: High Court Orders Notice to ED Over Gameskraft Founders Arrest
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has issued a notice to the Enforcement Directorate (ED) regarding the arrest of Gameskraft founders Deepak Singh, Prithvi Raj Singh, and Vikas Taneja. The founders are challenging their arrest in a money laundering case linked to allegations of luring players into real-money games under the guise of casual gaming. The case has been posted for a hearing on May 14.

articles.headline: AIADMK Rebels Support Vijay Ahead of Critical Floor Test
articles.summary: Rebel leaders from the AIADMK, led by SP Velumani and CV Shanmugam, have declared their support for C Joseph Vijay ahead of a critical floor test in the Tamil Nadu assembly. The rebels accused the party leadership of seeking support from the DMK and warned of a potential party split. Over 30 MLAs are reportedly part of the rebel camp supporting Vijay.

articles.headline: Udhayanidhi Stalin Remarks on Sanatan Dharma Stir Controversy
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin sparked a new controversy with his remarks on Sanatan Dharma during a speech in the state assembly. His comments, advocating for the 'eradication' of Sanatan Dharma, drew sharp criticism from the BJP and other political groups. Stalin defended his statement, saying it was directed against the practice of caste discrimination.

articles.headline: Paddy and Maize Acreage Expected to Shift to Oilseeds and Pulses
articles.summary: Farmers in India are expected to shift cultivation from paddy and maize toward oilseeds and pulses this year due to high prices and government support. The move is driven by a need to reduce dependence on edible oil imports and ensure better returns for farmers. Pulse prices are currently 15% higher than last year, making them an attractive alternative for the upcoming kharif season.

articles.headline: ED Arrests Two Former IDFC First Bank Employees for Money Laundering
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has arrested two former employees of IDFC First Bank in connection with a money laundering case involving ₹590 crore. The accused allegedly diverted public funds through various shell companies and fictitious bank accounts. The agency has seized assets worth several crores and is investigating the involvement of other bank officials in the scam.

articles.headline: Indian IT Stocks Tumble on Concerns Over OpenAI Adoptions
articles.summary: Shares of leading Indian IT companies fell up to 4.8% as investors reacted to OpenAI's latest AI advancements. The Nifty IT index hit its lowest close in three years amid fears that generative AI could disrupt traditional software service models. Tech Mahindra, LTM, and Persistent Systems were among the biggest losers as the industry faces structural challenges from AI adoption.

articles.headline: True North Exits Fedbank Financial Services with Stake Sale
articles.summary: Private equity firm True North has offloaded its entire remaining stake in Fedbank Financial Services for approximately ₹385 crore. The sale of 2.57 crore shares was conducted through a block deal on the stock exchanges. Following the exit, True North's representative on the company's board has resigned. Fedbank shares fell 1.9% following the news of the exit.

articles.headline: Anthropic Warns Investors Against Illegal Secondary Trade in Its Shares
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic has cautioned investors against unauthorized trading of its shares in secondary markets. The company stated that such trades violate its internal share transfer policies and may not be recognized by the firm. The warning comes as demand for shares in high-profile AI startups surges, leading to a rise in unregulated secondary market transactions.

articles.headline: Wall Street Falls from Records as AI Stocks Slump and Oil Rises
articles.summary: US stock indices fell from record highs as a slump in artificial intelligence stocks and rising oil prices dampened investor sentiment. The S&P 500 fell 1% while the Nasdaq Composite was down 2%. Major tech firms like Intel and Nvidia saw significant declines, while Brent crude prices rose to over $107 per barrel amid ongoing tensions in the West Asia region.

articles.headline: Core Income Expected to Support Canara Bank Profitability
articles.summary: Canara Bank expects its core income to provide stability to its profitability despite geopolitical tensions and volatile treasury income. The bank's net interest margin (NIM) was 2.51% for the last fiscal year and it aims to maintain it in the 2.5-2.6% range. The bank is focusing on rationalizing its cost of deposits and growing its retail and MSME loan portfolios to drive growth.

articles.headline: Indian Banks Expect Double-Digit Corporate Loan Book Growth This Year
articles.summary: Major Indian banks, including SBI, Bank of Baroda, and Canara Bank, expect their corporate loan books to grow by over 10% this fiscal year. The growth is expected to be driven by demand from sectors like renewable energy, infrastructure, thermal power, and data centers. Private sector banks also reported strong growth in their corporate loan portfolios in the last quarter.

articles.headline: Sebi Mandates Fifty Percent Cap on Portfolio Overlap for Mutual Funds
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has mandated that mutual fund schemes within the same fund house must limit their portfolio overlap to 50%. The move aims to ensure that different schemes from the same fund house provide genuine diversification for investors. Asset management companies have been given three years to comply with the new regulation.

articles.headline: ECLGS Extension Provides Relief to MSMEs Amid Global Headwinds
articles.summary: The extension of the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) has provided significant relief to MSMEs facing challenges from rising costs and global supply chain disruptions. Mid-sized banks with higher exposure to MSMEs have seen a 29-37% increase in their loan books under the scheme. The government's guarantee helps banks maintain their lending to smaller businesses.

articles.headline: Cement Companies Expect Costs to Dent Profit Growth
articles.summary: Indian cement companies expect their profit growth to be impacted by rising costs for fuel, packaging, and logistics. Demand for cement has also been muted due to unseasonal rains in some parts of the country. Major players like UltraTech and Ambuja Cement are focusing on cost-cutting measures and increasing their usage of alternative fuels to mitigate the impact on their margins.

articles.headline: Jamie Dimon Warns of Moving JPMorgan UK HQ Over Taxes
articles.summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned that the bank could move its UK headquarters out of London if taxes on the financial sector are raised too high. His comments come amid concerns about the potential economic policies of a future Labour government in the UK. Dimon emphasized the importance of a competitive tax environment for maintaining London's status as a global financial hub.

articles.headline: Amazon Sells Swiss Franc Bond Offering in Record Six Parts
articles.summary: Amazon.com sold its first-ever Swiss franc-denominated bond offering in six tranches to raise nearly $3.4 billion. The issue was oversubscribed as investors sought higher-yielding assets in a stable currency. The funds will be used for general corporate purposes, including potential acquisitions and investments in the company's various business segments.

articles.headline: Handset and Air Conditioner Prices Set to Rise in India
articles.summary: Prices for smartphones, televisions, and air conditioners in India are set to rise by 15-20% due to higher component costs and a weaker rupee. Manufacturers have already increased prices for some models to mitigate the impact of rising global commodity prices. The price hike is expected to impact consumer demand during the upcoming summer season and festive periods.

articles.headline: US Inflation Hits Over One-Year High on Oil and Food Costs
articles.summary: US retail inflation rose to its highest level in over a year in April, driven by surging costs for fuel, food, and housing. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 3.8% from a year earlier, exceeding economist expectations. The persistent inflationary pressure is likely to impact the Federal Reserve's decisions on interest rates and has raised concerns about a potential economic slowdown.

articles.headline: US War Costs in West Asia Reach Twenty Nine Billion Dollars
articles.summary: A senior Pentagon official reported that the United States has spent approximately $29 billion on military operations in the West Asia region over the last year. The costs include defense of Israel, anti-Houthi operations, and support for regional allies. The significant expenditure has sparked a debate in the US Congress over the long-term sustainability of the military's regional presence.

articles.headline: Trump Says He Will Discuss Taiwan and Jailed Tycoon with Xi
articles.summary: US Presidential candidate Donald Trump stated that he would discuss Taiwan's security and the case of jailed media tycoon Jimmy Lai with Chinese President Xi Jinping if elected. Trump emphasized his intention to maintain a strong stance on trade while seeking to improve overall relations with China. He expressed confidence in his ability to negotiate a favorable deal for the US.

articles.headline: UK's Keir Starmer Faces Challenges Amid Strong Political Current
articles.summary: Labour leader Keir Starmer is facing challenges from within his party as he prepares for a potential general election in the UK. Over 80 Labour MPs have expressed concerns about the party's direction on certain social and economic issues. Starmer is working to build a broad coalition of support while addressing the concerns of various party factions and the wider electorate.

articles.headline: Kevin Warsh Likely to be Voted onto Federal Reserve Board
articles.summary: The US Senate is expected to vote today on the confirmation of Kevin Warsh to the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Warsh is seen as a potential successor to Fed Chair Jerome Powell and his appointment is expected to have a significant impact on the central bank's future interest rate policies. Most Republican senators are expected to support his confirmation.

articles.headline: South Korean Policymaker Proposes Citizen Dividend from AI Taxes
articles.summary: A top South Korean policymaker has proposed the idea of a 'citizen dividend' funded by taxes on profits from artificial intelligence. The move aims to address potential job losses due to AI and ensure that the benefits of the technology are shared more widely. The proposal has sparked a debate in the country over the role of government in regulating and taxing emerging technologies.

articles.headline: French Parliament Summons TotalEnergies Chief Over Superprofits
articles.summary: The chief executive of French energy giant TotalEnergies has been summoned to testify before a parliamentary committee over the company's 'superprofits' during the energy crisis. Lawmakers are considering a windfall tax on the company's record earnings to help consumers with rising energy costs. TotalEnergies has defended its profits, saying they are necessary for investment in renewable energy.

articles.headline: US Colleges See Drop in Foreign Students Due to Visa Issues
articles.summary: The number of foreign students at US colleges has dropped by 20% this year, according to a report from a coalition of education groups. The decline is attributed to more stringent visa requirements and concerns about the political climate in the country. Many students are reportedly choosing to study in other countries with more favorable visa policies for international students.

articles.headline: Spain Reports New Hantavirus Case Bringing Total to Eleven
articles.summary: Spain has reported a new case of hantavirus, bringing the total number of cases in the country to 11. The patient, who is being treated in a hospital in Madrid, is in stable condition. Health officials are investigating the source of the infection and have urged the public to take precautions to avoid contact with rodents, which are known to carry the virus.

articles.headline: Donald Trump Announces Potential Aid Talks with Cuba
articles.summary: Donald Trump stated that his administration would be open to holding aid talks with the Cuban government if he is re-elected. He noted that the country is currently facing a severe economic crisis and needs international support. The announcement marks a potential shift in Trump's policy toward Cuba, which was characterized by increased sanctions and trade restrictions during his first term.

articles.headline: Google Co-founder Sergey Brin Launches Political War on California
articles.summary: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has reportedly launched a political campaign to oppose a proposed wealth tax in California. Brin and a group of other wealthy individuals have donated millions of dollars to political groups fighting the tax. The proposal, which would apply to individuals with more than $30 million in assets, has also been opposed by other tech leaders and venture capitalists.

articles.headline: Three Point Five Million Pages of Epstein Files on Exhibit
articles.summary: A new exhibit in New York City is featuring over 3.5 million pages of files related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The files, which were released by the US Department of Justice, include documents from several investigations and lawsuits involving Epstein and his associates. The exhibit aims to provide more information about Epstein's criminal activities and his high-profile connections.

articles.headline: Opera Based on Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera's Marriage
articles.summary: A new opera based on the tumultuous marriage of Mexican artists Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera is being staged at New York's Metropolitan Opera. The production, titled 'The Last Dream', features music by Mexican composer Gabriela Lena Frank and a libretto by playwright Nilo Cruz. The opera focuses on the couple's relationship and their impact on each other's work and lives.

articles.headline: Vodafone Idea Considers Raising Fresh Funds to Bolster Books
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea is reportedly considering raising up to ₹25,000 crore in fresh capital to improve its financial position and support its ongoing operations. The company is exploring several options for the fundraising, including a rights issue and a preferential share allotment. The move comes as the company continues to face high debt levels and intense competition in the Indian telecom market.

articles.headline: Retail Leasing Market in India Remains Strong Despite Supply Shortage
articles.summary: The retail leasing market in India remains strong, with high demand for space in top shopping malls and high streets. However, the market is facing a supply shortage, which has led to a rise in rental costs and lower vacancy rates. Major retailers are continuing to expand their presence in the country, but are finding it increasingly difficult to find suitable space for their new stores.

articles.headline: Hospitals Warn CGHS Reimbursement Cap May Hurt Cancer Treatment
articles.summary: Private hospitals in India have warned that a cap on reimbursements for cancer treatment under the Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS) could hurt patients. The hospitals stated that the cap is too low to cover the costs of several expensive cancer drugs and treatments. They have urged the government to increase the reimbursement rates to ensure that patients continue to receive high-quality care.

articles.headline: Electric Bike Manufacturers Put on Price Hike Path by Global Headwinds
articles.summary: Indian electric bike manufacturers are likely to increase prices for their products due to rising global costs for components and materials. The prices for several key parts, including batteries and motors, have increased significantly in recent months. The manufacturers have urged the government to provide more support for the industry to help them maintain competitive prices for their products.

articles.headline: Gold Jewellery Buyers Rush to Stock Up Ahead of Expected Price Hike
articles.summary: Jewellery buyers in India are rushing to stock up on gold ahead of an expected price hike in the coming weeks. The surge in demand has led to a rise in gold prices and a shortage of some popular jewellery items. Jewelers have reported a significant increase in sales, as buyers seek to lock in current prices before the expected hike takes place.

articles.headline: Travel Companies Call for Push for Domestic Tourism in India
articles.summary: Travel companies in India have called on the government to provide more support for the domestic tourism industry. The companies stated that the industry is facing several challenges, including high costs and a lack of infrastructure in some regions. They have urged the government to implement policies that would encourage more people to travel within the country and support local businesses.

articles.headline: Companies Seek Cyber Liability Insurance as AI Risks Surface
articles.summary: Indian companies are increasingly seeking cyber liability insurance as the risks associated with artificial intelligence and data breaches continue to grow. Insurance companies have reported a significant rise in inquiries for such policies, as companies look to protect themselves from potential financial losses and reputational damage. The policies provide coverage for a wide range of cyber risks.

articles.headline: Flipkart Delivery Operations Facing Eighteen Percent GST After Tax Ruling
articles.summary: Flipkart's delivery operations are facing an 18% Goods and Services Tax (GST) following a recent ruling by a tax body in West Bengal. The ruling found that the company's delivery services should be taxed at the higher rate, rather than the lower rate that the company had been using. The decision is expected to have a significant impact on the company's financial results and its delivery costs.

articles.headline: Lightspeed India Plans Smaller Fund with Three Hundred Million Dollar Corpus
articles.summary: Venture capital firm Lightspeed India is planning to launch a new fund with a smaller corpus of approximately $300-350 million. The move comes as the firm looks to focus its investments on more mature companies and startups with strong growth potential. The fund will be used to support companies in a wide range of sectors, including technology, healthcare, and consumer goods.

articles.headline: OpenAI to Cap Microsoft Revenue Payouts at Thirty Eight Billion Dollars
articles.summary: OpenAI is reportedly planning to cap its revenue payouts to Microsoft at $38 billion as part of a new agreement between the two companies. The move aims to ensure that OpenAI remains an independent company and is not overly dependent on Microsoft for its financial support. The agreement also includes provisions for the two companies to continue to work together on AI research and development.

articles.headline: Nazara Technologies Fourth Quarter Profit Up Thirteen Times Despite Lower Revenue
articles.summary: Indian gaming company Nazara Technologies reported a 13-fold increase in its net profit for the fourth quarter, despite a 23% decline in its revenue. The company's profit was boosted by lower operating costs and a one-time gain from the sale of several non-core assets. Nazara stated that it is focusing on its core gaming business and expects to see continued growth in the coming quarters.

articles.headline: Battle Over India's First Privacy Law Likely to Begin in Supreme Court
articles.summary: The legal battle over India's first comprehensive privacy law is likely to begin in the Supreme Court by the end of May. Several petitions have been filed challenging various provisions of the law, which critics say do not provide adequate protection for individuals' privacy. The government has defended the law, saying it provides a strong framework for protecting personal data and privacy.

articles.headline: Indian Companies Cut AI Outsourcing as they Adopt More AI In-house
articles.summary: Indian companies are reportedly cutting their outsourcing for AI-related services as they adopt more AI technologies in-house. The move aims to reduce costs and improve the overall efficiency of their operations. Companies are investing in their own AI capabilities and are hiring more AI experts to help them develop and implement new technologies across their various business segments.

articles.headline: Silicon Road Ventures and Ajay Mahajan Form Early Stage Investment Fund
articles.summary: Venture capital firm Silicon Road Ventures and veteran investor Ajay Mahajan have formed a new investment fund to support early-stage startups in India. The fund, which has a corpus of approximately ₹150 crore, will be used to provide seed funding for companies in a wide range of sectors, including technology, healthcare, and consumer goods. The fund aims to support the next generation of Indian entrepreneurs.

articles.headline: Fractal Analytics Fourth Quarter Profit Doubles to One Hundred Sixteen Crore
articles.summary: AI and analytics firm Fractal Analytics reported that its net profit for the fourth quarter doubled to ₹116 crore. The company's revenue also saw a significant increase, as more companies sought to adopt its AI and analytics solutions. Fractal stated that it is continuing to see strong demand for its services and expects to see continued growth in the coming year.

articles.headline: Uttar Pradesh Undergoes Major Transformation in Law and Order over Nine Years
articles.summary: The state of Uttar Pradesh has undergone a major transformation in its law and order situation over the last nine years, according to a government report. The report highlights several key achievements, including a significant reduction in crime rates and an increase in the number of successful prosecutions. The state government has also invested in new technologies to help improve law enforcement.

articles.headline: Electrifying India's Growth through New Technologies and Smart Infrastructure
articles.summary: India is on track to achieve a smarter, cleaner, and more electrified future through the adoption of new technologies and smart infrastructure. The transition is being driven by a combination of government initiatives and private sector investment, with a focus on improving the overall efficiency and sustainability of the country's power grid and transportation systems.

articles.headline: Smart Homes for Smart Living Redefine Modern Lifestyle in India
articles.summary: The adoption of smart home technologies is redefining modern lifestyles in India, as more people seek to improve the overall comfort and security of their homes. Smart home systems, including voice-activated controls and automated lighting and climate control, are becoming increasingly popular, as they offer a wide range of benefits for homeowners and their families.

articles.headline: Karnataka approves 12.5% pay hike for state transport employees
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has approved a 12.5% salary increase for employees of the state's four road transport corporations. The move benefits over one lakh workers and will result in an annual additional expenditure of Rs 873.6 crore. The revised dearness allowance (DA) is effective retrospectively from January 1, 2024.

articles.headline: Police investigate gelatin box found on Prime Minister's rally route
articles.summary: Bengaluru police are investigating a suspicious gelatin box found on the route of Prime Minister Modi's roadshow. While no immediate terror angle was found, the box contained batteries, wires, and a real-time clock. Three mobile phones were seized from a suspect in connection with the security lapse.

articles.headline: West Bengal to follow Assam model for border security
articles.summary: BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari announced that a future BJP government in West Bengal would adopt the 'Assam model' for border security. This involves a crackdown on illegal immigration, tighter frontier policing, and increased use of technology to secure borders with Bangladesh.

articles.headline: AIADMK faction eyes ministerial berths amid TVK non-committal stance
articles.summary: A faction of AIADMK led by Edappadi K Palaniswami is reportedly seeking 5 to 6 ministerial positions in the Tamil Nadu government. However, the TVK party, led by Vijay, has remained non-committal about joining the cabinet, despite rumors of an alliance and support for the ruling government.

articles.headline: Supreme Court says reforms can be brought through people's will
