articles.summary: The Supreme Court noted that constitutional courts should be cautious when intervening in matters of religious practices and traditions. The bench emphasized that significant reforms should ideally stem from the collective will of the people as expressed through their elected representatives in Parliament.

articles.headline: Dutch hospital staff quarantined after hantavirus protocol breach
articles.summary: A hospital in the Netherlands has quarantined 12 staff members as a preventive measure after they handled a patient with a hantavirus infection without following strict safety protocols. The patient reportedly passed away from the infection.

articles.headline: Nepal Supreme Court stays government's dissolution of trade unions
articles.summary: In a major legal setback for Nepal PM Balendra Shah, the Supreme Court has stayed the government's decision to dissolve civil service trade unions and student organizations. The stay order comes within the first 45 days of the Prime Minister's current term in office.

articles.headline: Hoax bomb threat emails outnumber calls in Karnataka
articles.summary: Data from the Karnataka police reveals that hoax bomb threat emails have surged significantly, outnumbering telephone hoaxes. Between January 2024 and April 2026, over 208 hoax email threats were registered compared to just 42 hoax calls, targeting schools, courts, and government offices.

articles.headline: Delivery worker stabbed by four men over honking in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A 23-year-old courier delivery man was stabbed by four men in Banaswadi after he honked at them to clear the road. The victim, Appu, was attacked after a brief verbal dispute. He is currently being treated in a hospital, and a case has been registered under the BNS Section 118.

articles.headline: Karnataka census deadline ends May 15 with 5.7 lakh vacant houses
articles.summary: The National Census deadline for Bengaluru is set for May 15, with officials reporting that 5.7 lakh houses remain vacant or unoccupied. As of the latest data, the census has covered 46.2 lakh out of 51.8 lakh total households, representing a coverage rate of 89.2%.

articles.headline: PWD engineer dies after jumping from hilltop in Karnataka
articles.summary: A 33-year-old Public Works Department (PWD) engineer, identified as Prasad, allegedly jumped to his death from the hilltop at Devarayanadurga. Police found his body 200 feet below a cliff after he had been missing for a day. An investigation into the circumstances of his death is ongoing.

articles.headline: Auto and taxi operators seek subsidies for CNG and EV shift
articles.summary: Bengaluru's autorickshaw and taxi operators have requested the state government to provide financial subsidies and interest-free loans to help them transition from LPG to CNG and electric vehicles. The move is aimed at reducing fuel costs and supporting cleaner transport alternatives.

articles.headline: Victim dies from burn injuries after mobile charger fire
articles.summary: A resident of Bengaluru succumbed to severe burn injuries after his mobile phone charger caught fire while he was sleeping. The victim, Nathan, reportedly left the device charging for over four hours, which led to a battery explosion that ignited his room's furniture.

articles.headline: Three caught attempting to sell illegal antler in Bengaluru
articles.summary: The Central Crime Branch has arrested three men for attempting to sell a deer antler near a college in Bengaluru. The suspects, hailing from Tamil Nadu and Karnataka, were caught with the illegal wildlife product and have been charged under relevant wildlife protection acts.

articles.headline: IIHR scientists develop new variety of Ganjam Chakkota fruit
articles.summary: Scientists at the Indian Institute of Horticultural Research (IIHR) have developed a new variety of Ganjam Chakkota (Pomelo), named Arka Siva. This new variety features pink flesh, fewer seeds, and a sweet orange-like flavor, aiming to revive interest in the local fruit across Bengaluru Rural and Chikkaballapur.

articles.headline: Shocked students dread NEET retest and switch to Engineering
articles.summary: Following the cancellation of NEET-UG 2026, many students in Karnataka are expressing frustration and anxiety over a potential retest. Reports indicate that several aspirants are now considering shifting their focus to engineering courses and the CUET exam to avoid the uncertainty of the medical entrance process.

articles.headline: Karnataka to install 1,243 high-capacity electric vehicle chargers
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has announced plans to set up 1,243 high-capacity EV charging stations across the state under the PM E-Drive scheme. The project, estimated to cost Rs 777 crore, aims to support the growing number of electric commercial vehicles and improve charging infrastructure.

articles.headline: Minister Madhu Bangarappa bats for decentralisation of NEET exam
articles.summary: Karnataka's Minister for Primary and Secondary Education, Madhu Bangarappa, has called for the decentralisation of the NEET exam. He suggested that states should be allowed to conduct their own medical entrance tests to avoid large-scale disruption and ensure better administrative control.

articles.headline: MM Hills pilgrimage trek limited to twice a year due to leopards
articles.summary: Following increased leopard activity and attacks in the Male Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary, the forest department has restricted pilgrimage treks to twice a year. The department will now only allow trekking during the Shivaratri and Ugadi festivals with strict safety protocols and forest guides.

articles.headline: Workers claim Rs 2 lakh unpaid wages in Dharmasthala search
articles.summary: Laborers involved in a multi-site search operation in Dharmasthala have alleged that their wages, totaling up to Rs 2 lakh, remain unpaid. The search was part of a SIT investigation into historical allegations of mass burials in the region. The workers have approached local authorities for resolution.

articles.headline: JD(S) considers Dalit and minority faces to counter TVK rise
articles.summary: Following the rapid political rise of Vijay's TVK in Tamil Nadu, the JD(S) in Karnataka is reportedly planning a structural overhaul. The party is considering elevating Dalit and minority leaders to key positions to strengthen its base and regain its distinct regional identity ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.headline: IT firms may push back on revised flexible work hour rules
articles.summary: Bengaluru's IT-BT industry bodies are preparing to oppose the central government's new notification on flexible work hours under the Occupational Safety and Health Code. The firms are concerned that the rules, which could require 12-hour shifts, might negatively impact employee welfare and operational costs.

articles.headline: Six Karnataka panchayats win national awards for rural development
articles.summary: Six gram panchayats from Karnataka have been selected for national awards under various categories by the Union Ministry of Panchayati Raj. The Mudradi GP in Udupi won in the 'Poverty-Free' category, while others were recognized for child-friendly and water-sufficient initiatives.

articles.headline: Karnataka government revises fees for land and survey records
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has updated the fee structure for obtaining copies of land and survey records. A circular issued by the Commissioner of SS&LR specifies new charges for village maps, atlas records, and boundary sketches, aimed at standardizing fee categories across all sub-registrar offices.

articles.headline: BJP to launch public campaign against Karnataka's reservation policy
articles.summary: The BJP in Karnataka is set to launch a statewide public campaign against the government's decision to roll back reservation hikes for SC and ST communities. Former CM Basavaraj Bommai termed the move a 'great injustice' and called for the restoration of the increased quotas implemented by the previous government.

articles.headline: Site for Bengaluru's second airport to be decided after study
articles.summary: The Karnataka government will finalize the location for Bengaluru's second international airport after a technical feasibility study is completed in October. Sites near Bidadi, Nelamangala, and Ramanagara are being considered to handle future traffic beyond the Kempegowda International Airport's capacity.

articles.headline: Yatnal seeks Lokayukta probe into Bengaluru waste tenders
articles.summary: Expelled BJP legislator Basanagouda Patil Yatnal has petitioned the Lokayukta to investigate alleged irregularities in solid waste management tenders in Bengaluru. Yatnal claimed that the process favored a particular contractor and resulted in inflated costs exceeding 19% of the estimated budget.

articles.headline: Dedicated cycle lanes made mandatory for major arterial roads
articles.summary: The Karnataka Motor Vehicle Rules have been amended to make dedicated cycle tracks mandatory on all major arterial roads with speed limits above 30kmph. The new rules also introduce special safety regulations and speed limits for school zones to protect pedestrians and cyclists.

articles.headline: Private schools body opposes state plan for mandatory online classes
articles.summary: An association representing private schools in Karnataka has opposed a government proposal to make online classes mandatory for primary school students. The body argued that prolonged screen time negatively impacts child development and that traditional classroom learning is essential for foundational years.

articles.headline: India asks China to introspect on reputational cost of aiding Pakistan
articles.summary: India has advised China to reflect on the reputational damage caused by its continued military and technical support to Pakistan, particularly during 'Operation Sindoor'. The Ministry of External Affairs stated that such support indirectly encourages cross-border terrorism and destabilizes regional security.

articles.headline: Navy chief-designate Admiral Tripathi says ready for cross-border threats
articles.summary: Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi, the incoming Chief of Naval Staff, has affirmed that the Indian Navy is fully prepared to counter any maritime threats from across the border. He emphasized the importance of maintaining a vigilant presence in the Indian Ocean to prevent infiltration and ensure national security.

articles.headline: RSS morning drill makes debut in Jadavpur University campus
articles.summary: In a historic first, the RSS conducted its morning drill (shakha) within the campus of Kolkata's Jadavpur University. The event, which took place near the Aurobindo Bhavan, was met with protests from left-leaning student unions but proceeded under heavy police presence.

articles.headline: Court orders JNU protesters to perform community service as punishment
articles.summary: A Delhi court has ordered a group of JNU students, involved in a 2017 protest, to perform 15 days of community service as a condition for discharging them from a criminal case. The students were also directed to submit an unconditional apology and maintain good behavior for one year.

articles.headline: TVK MLA barred from floor test following narrow one-vote victory
articles.summary: The Madras High Court has restrained a newly elected TVK MLA from participating in a legislative floor test until a petition challenging his narrow one-vote victory is resolved. The court noted that the participation of a disputed member could significantly impact the constitutional governance of the state.

articles.headline: India needs Rs 25 lakh crore for 100 GW nuclear energy
articles.summary: A report by TERI suggests that India will require an investment of Rs 23-25 lakh crore over the next two decades to reach a 100 GW nuclear energy capacity. The transition to small modular reactors (SMRs) is seen as a crucial step toward achieving long-term energy security and net-zero targets.

articles.headline: Kukis call to stop Manipur CM's visit to Churachandpur
articles.summary: Kuki tribal groups in Manipur have issued a call to block Chief Minister N Biren Singh's planned visit to Churachandpur. The groups stated that the visit would be seen as a provocation amid ongoing ethnic tensions and requested the CM to prioritize peace-building measures over official visits.

articles.headline: Jaw pain and clicking sounds can be early warnings of TMJ
articles.summary: Medical experts are warning that persistent jaw pain, clicking sounds while eating, and morning headaches could be early signs of Temporomandibular Joint (TMJ) disorders. The condition, which affects up to 25% of the population, is often linked to stress, teeth grinding, and poor posture.

articles.headline: Bioengineered eye tissue offers hope to address cornea shortage
articles.summary: Scientists at PGIMER Chandigarh have developed a breakthrough bioengineered cornea using discarded eye tissue. This innovative technique could potentially address the severe shortage of donor corneas in India, where over 70 lakh people suffer from corneal blindness.

articles.headline: CJI Kant: Courts will accept legislated social reforms
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that the judiciary is willing to accept significant social reforms if they are formally legislated by Parliament. He emphasized that while courts can interpret laws, the primary mandate for initiating social change lies with the elected representatives through the legislative process.

articles.headline: Whistleblower emailed leaked NEET paper to ANI after officials ignored warnings
articles.summary: A whistleblower in Rajasthan has revealed that they emailed the leaked NEET-UG question paper to several news agencies after education officials ignored initial warnings. The email, which contained images of the paper, was reportedly sent three days before the national examination took place.

articles.headline: Court: Breaking marriage promise no excuse for criminal behavior
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court has ruled that a man's failure to honor a promise of marriage cannot be used as an excuse for committing criminal acts against the woman. The court emphasized that such promises do not grant legal immunity for subsequent harassment or physical abuse.

articles.headline: NTA maintains zero tolerance policy even for single question match
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency has reiterated its strict policy that even if a single question from a leaked source matches the actual exam paper, the entire examination is compromised. This 'zero tolerance' stance led to the swift cancellation of the NEET-UG exam following the Bihar paper leak investigation.

articles.headline: Rationalists: Faith and conversion are matters of personal choice
articles.summary: A group of rationalists in a Supreme Court hearing argued that an individual's right to choose and practice any religion is protected by the Constitution. They stated that one should be free to change their faith at any time of the day without fear of legal or social repercussions.

articles.headline: Government departments explore measures to reduce fuel consumption
articles.summary: Various government departments are considering new measures to reduce fuel usage and government expenditure. Proposals include a 50% reduction in official car convoys and encouraging employees to use public transport or carpooling for their daily commutes.

articles.headline: West Bengal CM calls for Assam-style crackdown on illegal immigration
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee has expressed support for a stricter, Assam-style crackdown on illegal immigration along the state's borders. She emphasized the need for modern surveillance technology and increased coordination with central agencies to secure the frontier with Bangladesh.

articles.headline: US and Singapore envoys attend Assam CM's swearing-in ceremony
articles.summary: Ambassadors from the United States and Singapore were among the high-profile guests at the swearing-in ceremony of Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma. Their presence highlights the growing strategic and economic importance of Assam in India's 'Act East' policy and international trade.

articles.headline: Modi advises West Bengal BJP to avoid divisive comments
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has reportedly advised West Bengal BJP leaders to avoid making divisive or inflammatory comments. He emphasized the need for constructive politics and focused on development goals to win public trust following the party's recent electoral gains in the state.

articles.headline: Sikkim CM Prem Singh Tamang takes train to reach Guwahati
articles.summary: Sikkim Chief Minister Prem Singh Tamang won praise for his simplicity after he took a regular train to reach Guwahati for the oath-taking ceremony of the Assam CM. Tamang stated that his choice was intended to promote austerity and connect better with common citizens.

articles.headline: Hantavirus: Returning the favor? In '71 Iran sheltered Pakistan's assets
articles.summary: Declassified documents reveal that during the 1971 war, Iran provided a safe haven for Pakistani military assets, including several fighter jets. This historical disclosure comes amid shifting geopolitical dynamics as Iran and Pakistan seek to improve their current diplomatic and military cooperation.

articles.headline: Netanyahu mentions India's Prime Minister while discussing global unease
articles.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu cited his close relationship with India's PM Modi as a model for strong international partnerships during a press conference on the Gaza conflict. He emphasized that global leaders must stand together against regional threats to ensure long-term stability in the Middle East.

articles.headline: Pentagon estimates war in Iran would cost $29 billion so far
articles.summary: A leaked Pentagon report suggests that a hypothetical full-scale military conflict with Iran would have already cost the United States over $29 billion in operational and equipment expenses. The report underscores the massive economic burden of any new regional war in West Asia.

articles.headline: Trump's social media activity raises concerns about presidential focus
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump's frequent and late-night social media posts have sparked a debate about his focus and mental health ahead of the next election. Critics and supporters alike are analyzing the impact of his digital presence on national and international political discourse.

articles.headline: UAE denies hitting Iranian airbase as regional tensions rise
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates has officially denied reports that its forces were involved in a recent strike on an Iranian airbase. The UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that it remains committed to regional stability and diplomatic solutions despite the growing military friction in the Gulf region.

articles.headline: Kuwait claims Iran attacked island where China is building port
articles.summary: Kuwaiti authorities have alleged that Iranian forces attacked a strategically located island where China is currently assisting in the construction of a major commercial port. The incident has raised concerns about the safety of international infrastructure projects in the Persian Gulf.

articles.headline: Italy recognizes multi-parent family in landmark legal ruling
articles.summary: In a landmark decision, an Italian court has officially recognized a child's right to have three legal parents—two fathers and one mother. The ruling, which has sparked intense national debate, aims to protect the child's welfare and acknowledge contemporary family structures.

articles.headline: British PM Starmer remains defiant despite cabinet resignations
articles.summary: Despite several high-profile cabinet resignations and mounting calls for his departure, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer remains defiant. He stated that he has a mandate from his party to lead the country through current economic challenges and will not step down under political pressure.

articles.headline: Stock market falls 1,500 points amid Gaza ceasefire uncertainty
articles.summary: Indian stock markets experienced a sharp decline of 1,456 points (1.9%) as hopes for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip faded. The Sensex's fall was driven by fears of rising crude oil prices and continued global economic instability linked to the ongoing West Asian conflict.

articles.headline: India's GCCs to benefit as US proposes H-1B wage hikes
articles.summary: India's Global Capability Centers (GCCs) are expected to see a surge in growth following US proposals to significantly hike minimum H-1B wages. Experts suggest that higher costs in the US will lead global firms to offshore more high-skilled work to centers in cities like Bengaluru and Hyderabad.

articles.headline: April consumer price inflation in India hits 3.5%
articles.summary: India's consumer price-based inflation (CPI) edged up to 3.5% in April, largely driven by rising food and fuel costs. While core inflation remains below 4%, economists warn that persistent geopolitical tensions and high oil prices could put further upward pressure on household expenses.

articles.headline: Rising chip costs impact budget smartphone market in India
articles.summary: Increasing costs for semiconductor chips are hitting India's budget smartphone segment, with shipments for phones priced below Rs 10,000 dropping significantly. Manufacturers are reportedly struggling to maintain low price points while facing higher production costs and supply chain disruptions.

articles.headline: Nazara Technologies reports 13-fold profit growth despite revenue dip
articles.summary: Indian gaming firm Nazara Technologies reported a 13-fold increase in net profit for the fourth quarter, reaching Rs 52 crore. While total revenue saw a slight decline of 17%, the company's strong performance in esports and successful cost-optimization measures drove the record profit growth.

articles.headline: Ashish Limaye qualifies for Asian Games in equestrian event
articles.summary: Indian equestrian athlete Ashish Limaye has qualified for the upcoming Asian Games in the Eventing category. Limaye secured his spot after a strong performance at an international qualifying event in Poland, making him the first Indian to qualify in this discipline for the 2026 Games.

articles.headline: Shriyanshi Valishetty clinches Mexican International badminton title
articles.summary: 19-year-old Shriyanshi Valishetty has won the women's singles title at the Mexican International Challenge. She defeated Brazil's Juliana Viana Vieira in a hard-fought final (21-12, 21-16, 21-18), marking a significant milestone in her burgeoning international career.

articles.headline: PM Modi cuts motorcade size to promote government austerity
articles.summary: In a move to promote fiscal discipline, Prime Minister Modi has officially halved the size of his security motorcade. He has also directed all central government departments to adopt austerity measures, including encouraging staff to use public transport and avoiding high-cost official events.

articles.headline: Supreme Court: Primary school instruction should be in mother tongue
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that it is a constitutional mandate for state governments to ensure primary school instruction is provided in the child's mother tongue. The court emphasized that this is essential for cognitive development and protecting regional cultural identity.

articles.headline: IMD launches two new forecast services for Indian farmers
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has launched two new AI-driven forecast services specifically designed for farmers. These services provide hyperlocal weather information and sowing advice up to four weeks in advance, aiming to reduce crop loss and improve agricultural productivity.

articles.headline: NTA cancels NEET-UG 2026 after confirmed paper leak
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency cancelled the NEET-UG 2026 examination affecting 2.27 million students after confirmation that the question paper was compromised. Leaked papers were available on mobile phones as early as May 1. The CBI has taken over the investigation into a multi-state network. NTA Director General Abhishek Singh admitted institutional failure and promised a retest schedule soon.

articles.headline: Himanta Biswa Sarma sworn in as Assam CM for second term
articles.summary: BJP leader Himanta Biswa Sarma took the oath of office as Chief Minister of Assam for a second consecutive term in Guwahati. The ceremony was attended by PM Narendra Modi and Union ministers Amit Shah and Rajnath Singh. Four other ministers also took oath. The BJP-led NDA secured a historic mandate in the recent state elections, winning 102 out of 126 assembly seats.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi dissents on CBI director selection process citing lack of transparency
articles.summary: Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi submitted a strong dissent note during a high-powered panel meeting to select the next CBI director. Gandhi alleged he was not provided with crucial 360-degree reports on candidates, reducing the selection to a "mere formality." He stated that the LoP cannot be a "rubber stamp" in what he described as a biased exercise by the government.

articles.headline: Southwest monsoon expected to hit Andaman Islands this week
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced that the southwest monsoon is likely to arrive over the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and parts of the Bay of Bengal by the end of the week. This onset is ahead of the usual schedule. The monsoon is critical for the Indian economy, as nearly half of the country's net-sown area lacks irrigation and relies on the rain-bearing system.

articles.headline: India’s retail inflation rises to 3.48 percent in April
articles.summary: Headline retail inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index, rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March. This marks the fourth consecutive rise, driven primarily by food prices. Rural inflation stood higher at 3.74% compared to 3.16% in urban areas. Significant price hikes were noted in precious metals, with silver and gold jewellery seeing substantial year-on-year increases.

articles.headline: UK PM Keir Starmer rejects resignation calls despite election losses
articles.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer told his Cabinet he has no intention of resigning despite calls from within the Labour Party for leadership change. Several junior ministers stepped down following significant losses in local elections. While critics call for a timetable for his departure, no candidate has yet emerged to directly challenge Starmer's position.

articles.headline: CBI takes over probe into murder of Suvendu Adhikari aide
articles.summary: The CBI has taken over the investigation into the May 6 murder of Chandranath Rath, an aide to West Bengal leader Suvendu Adhikari. A seven-member team visited the crime scene in North 24 Parganas. Three suspects from Bihar and Uttar Pradesh were previously arrested by state police. Rath, a former IAF officer, was shot dead in his vehicle, sparking a political row between the BJP and TMC.

articles.headline: India launches $1.5 billion shipping insurance pool for war risks
articles.summary: The Indian government launched the $1.5 billion Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool (BMIP) with a $1.4 billion sovereign guarantee. The pool aims to facilitate continuous maritime insurance for Indian vessels navigating high-risk war zones, particularly in West Asia. This move ensures sovereign control over trade and protects against the withdrawal of foreign re-insurance due to sanctions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court committee to prepare roadmap for judicial infrastructure
articles.summary: Chief Justice Surya Kant has constituted a "Judicial Infrastructure Advisory Committee" to strengthen court facilities across India. The panel, chaired by Justice Aravind Kumar, will address the shortage of courtrooms, technological integration, and facilities for litigants. The move comes amid a decline in federal budgetary allocations for judicial infrastructure in the 2026-27 budget.

articles.headline: Four newborn cheetah cubs die at Kuno National Park
articles.summary: All four cheetah cubs born to KGP12 at Kuno National Park were found dead. Field officials believe a leopard attacked the one-month-old cubs, which were the country's first wild-born cheetahs. Their mother was found safe roaming nearby. The deaths highlight the challenges of the cheetah reintroduction project, as most surviving cubs at the park have been raised within enclosures.

articles.headline: Gujarat Titans reach top of IPL table after SRH win
articles.summary: Gujarat Titans moved to the top of the IPL standings with 16 points after defeating Sunrisers Hyderabad by 82 runs. Kagiso Rabada led the bowling attack with 3/28, dismissing SRH for just 86 runs. Earlier, Sai Sudarshan (61) and Washington Sundar (50) helped the Titans set a total of 168. The victory marks the Titans' fifth consecutive win in their quest for the playoffs.

articles.headline: WFI bars Vinesh Phogat from competition pending inquiry
articles.summary: Wrestling Federation of India president Sanjay Singh stated that Vinesh Phogat must wait for a committee decision on her show-cause notice before returning to competition. Phogat is currently ineligible for the Asian Games trials on May 30. The federation cited alleged indiscipline and rules regarding weight divisions. Phogat has slammed the WFI for blocking her comeback after the Olympics.

articles.headline: Hardeep Singh Puri Warns of Rs 2 Lakh Crore Oil Losses
articles.summary: Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri stated that oil marketing companies are losing Rs 1,000 crore daily due to high crude prices. Under-recoveries for the current quarter could reach Rs 2 lakh crore. Despite the crisis, India maintains a 60-day crude oil reserve. The Minister denied any link between fuel price decisions and elections, citing a 'real pressure test' for the economy.

articles.headline: Moody's Trims India's 2026 GDP Growth Forecast to 6.8%
articles.summary: Credit rating agency Moody's has lowered India's GDP growth forecast for 2026 from 7.6% to 6.8%. The forecast for 2027 was also cut to 6%. The agency attributed the reduction to a slowdown in private consumption, sluggish industrial activity, and high energy costs. Global macro outlook reports suggest energy price hikes will impact nations based on their energy resilience.

articles.headline: Public Sector Banks Post Record Profit of nearly Two Lakh Crore
articles.summary: India's public sector banks (PSBs) recorded a record net profit of ₹1.98 lakh crore in FY26, marking their fourth consecutive year of profitability. The aggregate operating profit rose to ₹3.21 lakh crore, while net non-performing assets (NPAs) fell to a low of 0.39%. The strong performance is attributed to improved asset quality, higher credit growth, and efficient management.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Record Low of 95.75 Against US Dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee plunged to a record low of 95.75 against the US dollar during intra-day trade before closing at 95.63. The currency has weakened by 6.02% this year. Pressures include high crude oil prices, consistent selling by foreign portfolio investors (FPI), and a surging dollar index. Brent crude prices near $107 per barrel have heightened concerns over India's import bill.

articles.headline: Uday Kotak Urges Caution Amid West Asia Conflict Shocks
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank founder Uday Kotak has warned that the impact of West Asian conflict on energy prices has not yet been fully felt by consumers. He urged the Indian industry to prepare for 'shocks' that will affect inflation and growth. Kotak supported PM Modi's appeal for austerity and advised businesses to move away from relying on petroleum-based energy and transport.

articles.headline: Dr. Reddy's Lab Reports 86% Decline in Q4 Net Profit
articles.summary: Pharma giant Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories (DRL) posted a consolidated net profit of Rs 220 crore for Q4FY26, down 86% from Rs 1,593 crore in the year-ago period. The slump was attributed to weak sales of its generic version of the cancer drug Revlimid in the US market. Operational revenue also fell 11% to Rs 7,516 crore. The board recommended a final dividend of Rs 5 per share.

articles.headline: India’s Global Market Cap Share Falls Following Recent Market Slide
articles.summary: India's share in the global equity market has fallen after recent declines in domestic indices. While the market capitalization reached a high in 2022, it has since seen a pullback due to persistent selling by foreign portfolio investors (FPIs). India currently ranks fifth in global market cap, but its share has slipped below the 3% threshold amid competition from other emerging markets.

articles.headline: RBI Cancels License of Sarvodaya Co-operative Bank
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has cancelled the license of Sarvodaya Co-operative Bank effective May 12, 2026. The regulator cited inadequate capital, poor earning prospects, and non-compliance with banking regulations. The bank is barred from all banking business. Depositors are eligible to receive up to Rs 5 lakh from the Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC).

articles.headline: BRICS Foreign Ministers to Meet in New Delhi on West Asia
articles.summary: New Delhi will host a two-day meeting of BRICS foreign ministers on May 14-15. The escalating crisis in West Asia and its impact on global energy supply chains will be the top agenda. While Iran's Foreign Minister confirmed attendance, China's Wang Yi will be represented by Ambassador Shi Feihong due to a clash with US President Trump's visit to Beijing.

articles.headline: April retail inflation hits 14-month high of 3.48%
articles.summary: Driven by a sustained rally in gold and silver prices, India's retail inflation accelerated to a 14-month high of 3.48% in April. While core inflation remained contained at 3.3%, food inflation for key items rose significantly. The figure remains below the RBI's 4% target but strengthens the case for maintaining current interest rates to ensure price stability.

articles.headline: CCI Approves Adani Power Proposal to Buy GVK Energy
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has approved Adani Power's proposal to acquire GVK Energy. The deal, valued at approximately ₹25,000 crore, includes Adani's acquisition of GVK's power plants in several states and its stake in several coal mines. The acquisition is expected to significantly strengthen Adani's position in the Indian power sector and expand its generation capacity.

articles.headline: War a Wake-up Call for Economic Self-Reliance Says Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated that the ongoing West Asia crisis serves as a wake-up call for India to accelerate its push for economic self-reliance. He urged industries to build resilient supply chains and reduce dependence on imports from a limited number of geographies. The minister emphasized the need to design and manufacture products in India to ensure long-term stability.

articles.headline: Kishore Biyani Fined for Future Retail Disclosure Lapses
articles.summary: Sebi has imposed a penalty of ₹20 lakh each on Kishore Biyani and his brother Rakesh Biyani for alleged disclosure violations related to Future Retail. The regulator found that the company had failed to disclose certain related-party transactions and and and failed to appoint an independent auditor to examine its accounts. The Biyanis have denied any wrongdoing in the case.

articles.headline: Sebi May Relax Norms for Select Agri Commodity F&O Contracts
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) is considering relaxing norms for certain agricultural commodity futures and options (F&O) contracts to improve liquidity. The regulator may allow cash settlement for some contracts and lower entry barriers for participants. The move aims to revive participation in agricultural derivatives, which has been low due to high costs and strict norms.

articles.headline: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi meets ministers to discuss India roadmap
articles.summary: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi met with Union Ministers Nirmala Sitharaman and Piyush Goyal to discuss the company's future investment roadmap in India. Discussions focused on the transition to electric vehicles, last-mile connectivity, and supporting the Gig economy. Khosrowshahi emphasized India's strategic importance as one of Uber's fastest-growing markets globally.

articles.headline: Advertising council proposes risk-based labelling of AI-generated ads
articles.summary: The Advertising Standards Council of India (ASCI) has proposed new guidelines for risk-based labelling of AI-generated content in advertisements. The draft rules aim to ensure transparency and prevent consumers from being misled by ultra-realistic AI images or deepfakes. Ads using high-risk AI features would require clear disclosure to maintain ethical advertising standards.

articles.headline: Policy Heads Brace for West Asia Economic Fallout and Macroeconomic Stress
articles.summary: Senior Indian officials, including the Chief Economic Adviser and Petroleum Minister, warned that the West Asia crisis poses a 'live balance of payments stress test.' During the CII annual summit, leaders emphasized the need for fiscal discipline, energy conservation, and infrastructure investment to counter rising inflation and currency depreciation risks.

articles.headline: eBay Rejects GameStop's Fifty Six Billion Dollar Acquisition Bid
articles.summary: Online marketplace eBay has rejected a $56 billion acquisition bid from video game retailer GameStop. The move comes as GameStop looks to expand its business and diversify its operations in the wake of declining sales for its physical stores. eBay stated that the bid was too low and did not adequately reflect the long-term value of the company and its future growth potential.

articles.headline: Rani Kapur Moves Supreme Court Over Family Trust Dispute
articles.summary: Industrialist Rani Kapur has moved the Supreme Court to stop her daughter-in-law Priya from interfering with the management of a family trust and several related companies. The dispute involves control over several crores of rupees in assets and investments. The court has issued a notice to all parties involved and is expected to hear the case later this month.

articles.headline: SBI Board Approves $2 Billion Long-Term Global Fundraising Plan
articles.summary: State Bank of India plans to raise up to $2 billion through overseas bond issuances this fiscal year. The bank aims to diversify its funding base via public offers or private placements of US dollar-denominated bonds. The move follows a previous $500 million borrowing in 2025 and seeks to tap global institutional investors amid favorable international market conditions.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu Government to Shut Liquor Shops Near Schools and Temples
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu government has announced the closure of 717 liquor shops located within 500 meters of schools, temples, and hospitals. The move follows a long-standing demand for prohibition in the state and aims to reduce the accessibility of alcohol. The state-run TASMAC operates over 4,700 retail outlets across Tamil Nadu, generating significant revenue for the government.
